evals.prompt: ok commit this, i've got the auth token refresh stuff staged already. recent commits are like feat(auth): add login flow, fix(api): rate limit headers, etc.
evals.expected_output: Reads git diff --cached to understand the staged changes, composes a conventional commit message matching the repo style (e.g. feat(auth): add token refresh logic), and executes git commit via HEREDOC without asking the user to confirm the message first. After commit, attempts to archive plans and suggests push.
expectations: Does NOT ask the user to confirm or approve the commit message — proceeds directly from analysis to git commit
Generates a conventional commit message (type(scope): description) that matches the repo's existing style
Uses HEREDOC format for the git commit command

evals.prompt: just ran /commit and the pre-commit hook rejected it — eslint says 'no-unused-vars' on line 15 of src/utils.ts. the commit didn't land.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the commit never landed. Shows the lint error, explains the unused variable issue, and suggests a fix. After fix, creates a NEW commit — does not use --amend since the original commit never existed.
expectations: Does NOT use --amend, because the original commit never landed — amending would modify the previous commit
After fixing the lint issue, creates a NEW commit rather than amending
Shows the error and explains the issue before attempting any fix

evals.prompt: nothing is staged but i want to save my work, i changed a bunch of files across the codebase. use --auto
evals.expected_output: Since --auto is specified and nothing is staged, runs git add -u to stage all tracked changes without asking. Then reads the diff, composes a conventional commit message summarizing the changes, and commits.
expectations: Stages tracked changes with git add -u (not git add -A which would include untracked files)
Does not ask what to stage because --auto was specified
Reads the diff to compose a meaningful commit message rather than a generic one
